content: Just as state-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agent memory systems prioritize contextual retention of high-uncertainty, curiosity-triggered experiences to improve future sample efficiency, a general agent’s functional identity can be reframed as a curated memory prioritization queue that continuously prunes low-information past interactions to make space for novel, learning-critical data. This reframing flips the default view of agents as static task executors into dynamic, memory-shaped learners.
